zh.lifeSummary: 當Alan de Heaton Lord of Ellingham於1315年出生在英國諾圖波藍德林加姆時，他的父親Thomas de Heaton II27歲，母親Agnes de Heaton23歲。他於大約1336年與Lady Constance De Leybourne結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1387年3月21日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德林加姆享年72歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德林加姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Heaton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from any of various places called with Old English hēah ‘high’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’: Heaton (Yorkshire, Northumberland); Heaton Norris, Heaton under Horwich, Heaton with Oxcliffe, Great Heaton, Little Heaton (all Lancashire); Capheaton, Kirkheaton (Northumberland); Cleckheaton, Hanging Heaton, Kirkheaton (Yorkshire). This surname was taken to Ireland in the mid 17th century, and within Ireland is now mainly found in Ulster.

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Alan de Heaton Lord of Ellingham was born in 1315, in Chillingham, Northumberland, England, his father, Thomas de Heaton II, was 27 and his mother, Agnes de Heaton, was 23. He married Lady Constance De Leybourne about 1336. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 21 March 1387, in Chillingham, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 72, and was buried in Chillingham, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alan de Heaton Lord of Ellingham est né en 1315, à Chillingham, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Heaton II, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Heaton, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Lady Constance De Leybourne environ 1336. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 21 mars 1387, à Chillingham, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Chillingham, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alan de Heaton Lord of Ellinghamが1315年にイギリスノースウンバーランドチリンガムで生まれた時，父Thomas de Heaton IIは27歳，母Agnes de Heatonは23歳であった。約1336年にLady Constance De Leybourneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1387年3月21日にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドチリンガムで72歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドチリンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alan de Heaton Lord of Ellingham(이)가 1315년 영국 노르스벌랜드 치링함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Heaton II(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Heaton(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1336년 Lady Constance De Leybourne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1387년 3월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 치링함에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 치링함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alan de Heaton Lord of Ellingham nasceu em 1315, em Chillingham, Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Heaton II, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Heaton, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Constance De Leybourne aproximadamente 1336. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 21 de março de 1387, em Chillingham, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Chillingham, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alan de Heaton Lord of Ellingham родился в 1315 г. в местности чиллингхам, нортумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas de Heaton II было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Heaton, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Constance De Leybourne около 1336 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 21 марта 1387 г. в местности чиллингхам, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности чиллингхам, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания.
